Off Calendar May-15-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. ANDRE WON, Esq. (tel: 650-245-6457), representing himself.
Atty. JAMES MILLAR, Esq. of Millar Law Firm, APLC (tel: 415-981-8100) for Defendants.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS JAMES DAVIS, AND SAMANTHA DAVIS DOOLITTLE's Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery.

Matter discussed. Upon conclusion of discussion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling with modifications to correct the misspelling of Mr. Won's name.

The unopposed motion by defendants and cross-complainants Samantha Davis Doolittle and James Davis for responses to discovery, a deemed-admitted order, and sanctions against cross-defendant Andrew Won is granted. The Millar declaration establishes that the Davises served form interrogatories, requests for production of documents, and requests for admissions and Won has not responded. By failing to respond he has waived objections. (See CCP 2030.290(a), 2031.300(a), 2033.280(a).) The court orders Won to serve verified, objection-free responses to the form interrogatories and requests for production within 15 days of entry of this order. The matters subject to the requests for admission are deemed admitted by Won. (CCP 2033.280(b), (c).)

The Davises seek sanctions. Sanctions are appropriate on a discovery motion where the objecting party does not act with substantial justification in its discovery compliance, even where she does not oppose the motion. (See CCP 2029.290(c); id. 2031.300(c); Rule of Court 3.1348(a).) Sanctions are mandatory where a party fails to respond to requests for admission. (CCP 2033.280(c).) The court orders Won to pay sanctions but reduces the amount to reflect that no reply brief was needed. Won shall pay $1060 to the Davises within 15 days of entry of this order.

Order signed.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Off Calendar Mar-14-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Atty. ANDREW WON, Esq. (via telephonic appearance) (tel: 650-245-6457) for Plaintiff.
Atty. JAMES MILLAR, Esq. (via Zoom videoconference) of Millar Law Firm, APLC (tel: 415-981-8100) for Defendants.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS JAMES DAVIS, AND SAMANTHA DAVIS DOOLITTLE's Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Admissions And Production.

The Court passes the matter to allow counsel Andrew Won to appear. The Court recalls the matter. Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Moving parties James Davis and Samantha Davis Doolittle ("Davises") seek an order compelling responding party FanX, Inc. to respond to its Requests for Production of Documents and Requests for Admission. The moving papers demonstrate that the Davises served this discovery on FanX, FanX did not timely respond, and FanX has not yet responded. By failing to respond, FanX has waived its objections to the requests. (CCP 2031.300(a); 2033.280(a).)

FanX opposes this relief on the grounds that this court's order of November 2, 2023, denying arbitration was erroneous. FanX avers that it has sought writ review of the November 2 order, and in the interim it acted with substantial justification in not participating in discovery. This argument is misplaced. Regardless of a party's belief in the correctness of an order, it must follow the order unless the party has sought and obtained a stay of the litigation from the trial court or has obtained a stay of the litigation or reversal of the order from the appellate court.

The court orders as follows: the matters specified in the Davises' Requests for Admission, Set One, are deemed admitted by FanX. (CCP 2033.230(b), (c).) FanX is ordered to provide code-compliant responses to the Requests for Production, without objections, within 20 days of notice of entry of this order. FanX must complete its document production within 45 days of notice of this order. No objections may be asserted.

The court shall impose monetary sanctions on a party who unsuccessfully opposes a motion to compel responses to discovery unless it finds that the party acted with substantial justification or other circumstances make imposition of sanctions unjust. (CCP 2031.300(c).) It is mandatory that the court award sanctions where a party fails to respond to requests for admissions. (CCP 2033.280(c).) The court does not find that FanX acted with substantial justification here and imposes the requested amount. FanX shall pay $1860 to the Davises in sanctions within 15 days of notice of entry of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling, with a cover letter reflecting compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

The court finds SMI's sanctions request reasonable and awards $1,185 in sanctions, payable by FanX to SMI. The sanctions shall be paid within 15 days of notice of entry of this order.
